content.cn_text: 第三部分。
content.en_text: Section 3

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第21至第25题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第25题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: Tutor：嗨，Frances，Sam。
content.en_text: Tutor: Hi Frances, Sam.

content.cn_text: 那么，我让你们写的报告中，不同理论商业工具的应用进展怎么样？
content.en_text: So, how did you go with applying the different theoretical business tools for the report I asked you to write?

content.cn_text: Frances：挺有意思的，也让我们真正思考分析企业时哪种工具最适合。
content.en_text: Frances: It was interesting and made us really focus on which tool was best to use when analysing a business.

content.cn_text: Tutor：很好，那告诉我你们发现了什么。
content.en_text: Tutor: Good, so tell me what you found out.

content.cn_text: Frances: 我们喜欢 PEST 分析背后的理论。
content.en_text: Frances: We liked the theory behind PEST analysis.

content.cn_text: 它指的是政治、经济、社会和技术因素，不过我们不确定它是否很适用于我们的案例。
content.en_text: That's political, economic, social and technological, although we're not sure if it was very applicable to our case.

content.cn_text: 其他一些小组的研究很适合用它，而且他们说它很容易使用。
content.en_text: Some of the other groups had studies for which it worked really well and they said it was easy to use.

content.cn_text: 但我觉得它过于关注宏观层面，不适合我们的公司。
content.en_text: But I felt it focused too much on the big picture and was unsuitable for our company.

content.cn_text: Sam：我同意。另一方面，我喜欢使用 Drill Down 方法。
content.en_text: Sam: I agree. On the other hand, I enjoyed using the Drill Down method.

content.cn_text: 做起来很费力，而且我们似乎花了很久时间。
content.en_text: It was painstaking to do and we seemed to be working on it for ages.

content.cn_text: 但结果值得等待。
content.en_text: But the results were worth waiting for.

content.cn_text: Frances:：是的，我喜欢它最终能把复杂问题分解开来。
content.en_text: Frances:: Yes, I liked the way it eventually broke down complex problems.

content.cn_text: Sam：是的，我更喜欢做 PMI 分析。
content.en_text: Sam: Yes, I much preferred doing the PMI analysis.

content.cn_text: 它很直接，可以分成三个部分：优点、缺点和有趣影响。
content.en_text: It was so straightforward to break down into its three components, plus, minus and interesting implications.

content.cn_text: 你只需要对这些部分进行头脑风暴，然后写出来，快速又有效。
content.en_text: You just needed to brainstorm these components and then write them up, fast and effective.

content.cn_text: 而且做起来不会让人头疼。
content.en_text: And it didn't make your brain hurt doing it.

content.cn_text: Frances:：我同意。而且完全不需要特殊培训，任何人都能用。
content.en_text: Frances:: I agree. And you don't need any special training at all. Anyone can use it.

content.cn_text: Tutor：那么，你们认为有没有哪种工具比其他工具更好？
content.en_text: Tutor: So was there any tool that you thought was superior to the others?

content.cn_text: Frances：在我看来，对企业尤其是大公司至关重要的一种工具是 Pareto 分析。
content.en_text: Frances: In my opinion, the one tool that is critical to business, especially for larger companies, is Pareto Analysis.

content.cn_text: 如果他们应用 Pareto 分析，就会发现通过关注关键的20%问题，
content.en_text: If they apply Pareto Analysis, they would see that by focusing on the critical 20% of their problems,

content.cn_text: 他们可以产生80%的收益。
content.en_text: they could generate 80% of the benefits.

content.cn_text: 这能让他们工作得更快，同时也减少工作量。
content.en_text: This would allow them to work faster and it also reduces their workload.

content.cn_text: 它的核心就是找出他们的基本问题。
content.en_text: It's all about finding out what their basic problems are.

content.cn_text: Sam：我同意 Frances 关于 Pareto 的看法，但我最喜欢的是 SWOT 分析。
content.en_text: Sam: I agree with Frances about Pareto, but my favourite was the SWOT Analysis.

content.cn_text: 也就是分析公司的优势、劣势、机会和威胁。
content.en_text: You know, looking at the company's strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats.

content.cn_text: 因为它可以用于很多不同情况，适用于大公司和小公司，甚至可以用到部门层面。
content.en_text: As it can be used in so many different situations, it works in large and small companies and even down to departmental level.

content.cn_text: 它非常适合我们的案例研究。
content.en_text: It was great for our case study.

content.cn_text: Frances：是的，没错。
content.en_text: Frances: Yes, that's true.

content.cn_text: 在听讨论的剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第6页的第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 26-30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第6页的第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 26-30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第26至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 26-30.

content.cn_text: Tutor：好的，我已经读了你们的报告草稿，但你们能再多告诉我一些你们关注的那家公司吗？
content.en_text: Tutor: Okay, I've read your draft report, but can you tell me a bit more about that company you focused on?

content.cn_text: Frances：嗯，我们研究的是一家制造公司，它一直依靠经销商把产品送到最终用户手中。
content.en_text: Frances: Well, the company we used in our study was a manufacturing company who have relied on distributors to take their product to the end user.

content.cn_text: 我们做了一些分析，研究他们自行分销产品的可能性。
content.en_text: We did some analysis examining the possibility of them distributing their own products.

content.cn_text: Sam: 我们最初研究了他们的优势。这家公司虽然规模小，但很有名，而且声誉很好。
content.en_text: Sam: We initially looked at their strengths. The company, although it is small, is well known and has a very good reputation.

content.cn_text: 管理层非常致力于增加公司利润，并且他们相信这一举措会成功。
content.en_text: The management are very committed to increasing the company profits and they are confident this move would be successful.

content.cn_text: Frances:对，而且这家公司有相当多员工以前在分销公司工作过，所以相关知识已经具备。
content.en_text: Frances: Right, and the company has quite a lot of staff who have previously worked for distribution companies, so the knowledge is already there.

content.cn_text: 这是我们没想到会发现的，而且这可能最能帮助他们实现目标。
content.en_text: That was something we didn't expect to find and is probably going to help them the most to achieve their goals.

content.cn_text: Tutor：你们详细说明的一些机会让我印象很深。
content.en_text: Tutor: I was quite impressed by some of the opportunities you detailed.

content.cn_text: 不过，我确实认为你们漏掉了一个与他们未来增长非常相关的因素。
content.en_text: I did think you missed something quite pertinent to their future growth, however.

content.cn_text: Sam：是什么？
content.en_text: Sam: What was that?

content.cn_text: Tutor：你们没有考虑建立海外部门的可能性。
content.en_text: Tutor: You didn't consider the potential for establishing an offshore division.

content.cn_text: 你们谈到了公司与供应商谈判以获得更好条件。
content.en_text: You talked about the company negotiating better terms with its suppliers.

content.cn_text: 这些供应商大多数在海外，所以如果公司能在那里实际分销产品，
content.en_text: Most of these are based abroad, and so if the company had a way of actually distributing the product there,

content.cn_text: 这将是一个巨大的机会，并让他们比竞争对手更有优势。
content.en_text: this could be a tremendous opportunity and give them an edge over their competitors.

content.cn_text: Frances: 这是个好点。
content.en_text: Frances: That's a good point.

content.cn_text: 那您觉得我们识别出的威胁怎么样？
content.en_text: So what did you think about the threats we identified?

content.cn_text: Tutor：我觉得你们做得不错。
content.en_text: Tutor: I thought you did a good job.

content.cn_text: 我很高兴看到你们考虑了政府计划中的环保政策会如何真正影响这个项目的可行性。
content.en_text: I was really pleased to see that you considered how the government's planned environmental policy could really affect the viability of this venture.

content.cn_text: 随着这项法律明年出台，他们可能需要寻找使用现有技术的新方法。
content.en_text: With this law being introduced next year, they might need to find new ways of using their existing technology.

content.cn_text: 识别竞争对手在做什么很容易，但更宏观的情况常常被忽略。
content.en_text: It's easy to identify what your competitors are doing, but it's the bigger picture that often gets ignored.

content.cn_text: 那么 Sam，你从这次作业中学到了什么？
content.en_text: So Sam, what did you learn from this assignment?

content.cn_text: Sam：很多。
content.en_text: Sam: Heaps.

content.cn_text: 我想我以前没有意识到，要确保公司生存并盈利需要花这么多时间。
content.en_text: I guess I hadn't realized how much time it takes to ensure your company survives and profits.

content.cn_text: 在书本中学习不同工具很有用，但在现实世界中应用它们比我想象的难得多。
content.en_text: It was great to study the different tools in books, but applying them in the real world was much harder than I thought.

content.cn_text: 这是一次很棒的学习经历。
content.en_text: It was a great learning experience.

content.cn_text: Tutor：我很高兴能让你们的作业有实际意义。
content.en_text: Tutor: I'm glad I could make your assignment relevant.

content.cn_text: 我基本上对你们的报告满意。
content.en_text: I'm basically happy with your report.

content.cn_text: 你们的格式是正确的，有必要的小标题等等。
content.en_text: You've got the format correct, with the necessary headings, etc.

content.cn_text: 而且不同标题下的细节也足够。
content.en_text: And you've got sufficient details under the different headings.

content.cn_text: 只要记得说明你们是否认为公司应该推进这个新项目。
content.en_text: Just remember to state whether or not you think the company should go ahead with a new venture.

content.cn_text: 不过到目前为止，你们做得很好。
content.en_text: But you've done a good job so far.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 3.

content.cn_text: 现在你有半分钟检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter from A–G
groups.title: Questions 21–25

list.title: A
list.content: it will save a lot of business time and effort

list.title: C
list.content: it does not fit our company

list.title: G
list.content: it is suitable to almost all sized companies

items.title: PEST
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 PEST 分析方法的特点。原文说 it focused too much on the big picture and was unsuitable for our company，所以答案是 C. it does not fit our company。

items.title: Drill Down
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Drill Down 方法的特点。原文说 it was painstaking，并且 working on it for ages，说明耗时很长，所以答案是 D. it will take too long。

items.title: PMI
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问 PMI 分析方法的特点。原文说 it was straightforward，fast and effective，并且 anyone can use it，所以答案是 E. it is easy to use。

items.title: Pareto
items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问 Pareto 分析方法的特点。原文说它能 allow them to work faster and reduces their workload，所以答案是 A. it will save a lot of business time and effort。

items.title: SWOT
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问 SWOT 分析方法的特点。原文说 it works in large and small companies and even departmental level，所以答案是 G. it is suitable to almost all sized companies。

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 26–30

items.title: What does Frances consider as the best strength of the company?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 Frances 认为这家公司最大的优势是什么。虽然前面提到 reputation 和 management，但后面说有很多员工有分销经验，the knowledge is already there，并且 will help them the most，所以答案是 B. experienced employees。

items.title: What factor did Sam overlook for the future growth of the company?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问 Sam 忽略了哪个有利于公司未来增长的因素。原文说 didn't consider establishing an offshore division，对应 B. set up an overseas office。

items.title: Which of the following can be the threat to a company?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问哪一项可能成为公司的威胁。原文说政府 planned environmental policy，并且 this law being introduced next year 会影响项目可行性，所以答案是 C. new legislation。

items.title: What has Sam learned from the research?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问 Sam 从研究中学到了什么。原文说 studying tools in books was great, but applying them in the real world was much harder，说明学到了理论与现实之间的差距，所以答案是 C. gap between reality and theory。

items.title: What is the professor’s suggestion for the report?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问教授对报告的建议是什么。原文说 remember to state whether or not you think the company should go ahead，也就是给出最终判断，所以答案是 A. give a final determination。
